faqs.answer: The stipend is tax-free and non-reportable, so it shouldn't affect Social Security, Medicare, or housing assistance. However, confirm this with your parent's benefits administrator to be certain.

faqs.question: What if my parent's income is just over the limit?
faqs.answer: Out-of-pocket medical expenses can be deducted from income to meet eligibility standards. If your parent has high medical costs, mention this when you call.

faqs.question: Does my parent need special training to be a companion?
faqs.answer: Yes, the program provides orientation and ongoing training. No prior experience needed — they'll learn everything required, including how to support homebound adults safely.

faqs.question: What if my parent doesn't want to transport clients in their car?
faqs.answer: Transportation of clients may be required, so your parent needs reliable transportation and auto insurance. Ask the coordinator if non-driving positions are available.

faqs.question: Can my parent choose their schedule within the 10-hour minimum?
faqs.answer: Visits are only available Monday–Friday, 8:30 a.m.–4:30 p.m., but the coordinator can work with your parent's availability within those hours.

faqs.question: What counties does this program serve?
faqs.answer: The program operates in Belknap, Grafton, Hillsborough, Merrimack, Rockingham, Strafford, and Sullivan counties.

programs.name: Monadnock at Home Community Services
programs.tagline: One phone call connects your parent to vetted home care providers, transportation, and daily support in New Hampshire's Monadnock region.

localEntities.name: Program of Catholic Charities NH, serving Monadnock Region towns.

programs.intro:
If your parent lives in one of 12 towns around Mount Monadnock and wants to stay in their home longer, this membership program connects them to everything they need through one phone call. Because Monadnock at Home vets all providers in advance, your parent gets trusted help with home maintenance, transportation to medical appointments, grocery delivery, and companion visits without you having to research each service separately.

The program covers Dublin, Fitzwilliam, Greenfield, Hancock, Harrisville, Jaffrey, Marlborough, New Ipswich, Peterborough, Rindge, Sharon, and Temple. Your parent pays a nominal membership fee and can then access volunteers for basic home maintenance, a network of vetted home health aides and repair services, weekly grocery trips, medication delivery, and regular check-in calls. Because it's run by Catholic Charities NH with local hospital partnerships, the service coordination comes from established community connections.

summary: Lives in one of 12 Monadnock region towns
Seeking to maintain independence at home
Able to benefit from membership services
otherRequirements: Must reside in Dublin, Fitzwilliam, Greenfield, Hancock, Harrisville, Jaffrey, Marlborough, New Ipswich, Peterborough, Rindge, Sharon, or Temple

applicationGuide.summary: Submit contact form online or call Catholic Charities NH to request membership enrollment.

steps.title: Check town eligibility
steps.description: Confirm your parent lives in one of the 12 covered towns: Dublin, Fitzwilliam, Greenfield, Hancock, Harrisville, Jaffrey, Marlborough, New Ipswich, Peterborough, Rindge, Sharon, or Temple.

steps.title: Submit contact form
steps.description: Go to the Catholic Charities NH website and click 'request help' on the Monadnock at Home page to submit initial contact information.

steps.title: Complete membership enrollment
steps.description: Staff will contact you to complete membership enrollment and explain the nominal fee structure.

steps.title: Access services
steps.description: Once enrolled, your parent can call the program directly to coordinate any needed services through their vetted provider network.

applicationGuide.tip: Because this covers a small geographic area, services may have limited availability during peak times.

offices.name: Monadnock at Home (Catholic Charities NH)
offices.address: Serves Dublin, Fitzwilliam, Greenfield, Hancock, Harrisville, Jaffrey, Marlborough, New Ipswich, Peterborough, Rindge, Sharon, Temple

categories.name: Residency
items: Proof of residence in eligible towns (utility bill, lease, or mail)

contentSections.text: This program only serves 12 specific towns in the Monadnock region. If your parent lives outside these towns, they won't be eligible regardless of other factors.

faqs.question: What's the membership fee and when does Mom pay it?
faqs.answer: The program charges a nominal membership fee, but the specific amount isn't publicly listed. You'll learn the fee amount when staff contacts you after you submit the initial request form.

faqs.question: Can Dad use this if he lives in Keene or other nearby towns?
faqs.answer: No. The program only serves 12 specific towns: Dublin, Fitzwilliam, Greenfield, Hancock, Harrisville, Jaffrey, Marlborough, New Ipswich, Peterborough, Rindge, Sharon, and Temple. Keene and other surrounding towns aren't covered.

faqs.question: How quickly can Mom get help with home maintenance or transportation?
faqs.answer: Response time isn't specified, but because this serves a small geographic area, availability may be limited during busy periods. The program uses both volunteers and vetted providers, so timing depends on what type of help she needs.

faqs.question: Does insurance or Medicaid cover any of the membership fee?
faqs.answer: This isn't a government program, so Medicare and Medicaid won't cover the membership fee. It's a private membership service run by Catholic Charities NH.

faqs.question: What if Mom needs more medical care than basic home help?
faqs.answer: The program includes access to vetted home health aides and partners with Monadnock Community Hospital, but it's primarily for maintaining independence, not intensive medical care. For complex medical needs, you'd need separate home health services.

stateOverview.intro: New Hampshire offers 18 programs to help your aging parent stay safe, fed, and independent at home. These range from financial benefits like SNAP grocery assistance ($415/month for couples) and heating bill help ($94–$2,177 through LIHEAP) to comprehensive health coverage through Medicaid and home care services. Most programs have income limits around $2,000–$4,000/month, but several valuable resources — like Medicare guidance through SHIP and legal help — are free to all seniors regardless of income. Start by checking if your parent qualifies for Medicaid, which opens doors to the most comprehensive services including home care that can prevent nursing home placement.

startHere.name: Medicaid for Aged, Blind, Disabled (ABD)
startHere.why: Comprehensive health coverage plus home care services — the foundation that unlocks other supports.

startHere.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
startHere.why: Quick financial relief for grocery costs that most fixed-income seniors can access.

startHere.name: SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
startHere.why: Free expert help to ensure your parent isn't overpaying for Medicare coverage.

startHere.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
startHere.why: No income limits — get respite care and support services regardless of your parent's financial situation.

byNeed.need: Help paying for healthcare and home care
programs: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualified Individual (QI/SLMB-135)
byNeed.description: Comprehensive health coverage and services to help your parent stay at home instead of entering a facility.
Monthly grocery money, heating bill help, and free home-delivered meals for parents on fixed incomes.

programs: Easterseals NH In-Home Care & Health Services
byNeed.description: Professional care services when your parent needs daily help but wants to remain independent.

programs: Caregiver Respite and Senior Volunteer Programs
byNeed.description: Get temporary care relief and support services when you need a break from caregiving.
Expert guidance on Medicare, legal issues, and facility problems — no income requirements.
Free energy improvements to reduce heating costs and part-time work opportunities for active seniors.

quickFacts: Most New Hampshire benefit programs use income limits between $1,660–$3,967/month for single seniors
New Hampshire does not yet have PACE (all-inclusive care) programs available, unlike neighboring states
Several programs require no income limits: Family Caregiver Support, SHIP counseling, and legal assistance are free to all
Medicaid eligibility opens access to the most comprehensive services including CFI Waiver home care
stateOverview.resourcesVsBenefits: New Hampshire programs fall into two categories: benefits that require income qualification (like SNAP grocery assistance or Medicaid health coverage) and free resources available to everyone regardless of income. For example, SHIP provides free Medicare counseling to any senior, while Legal Assistance for Older Adults helps with housing disputes and benefit appeals at no cost. Even if your parent's income is too high for financial benefits, they can still access expert guidance and advocacy services.
